title: Outdoor Planter Steps or Benches
summary: A two-tier 2x8 platform built to work as garden steps or a stacked bench — 45°-mitered 2x4 top trim and 2x6 treads dress up a genuinely simple stacked-box structure.
description:
This is two simple 2x8 boxes stacked on each other, sized so the top step sits back far enough from the bottom step's edge to actually be walkable — or, since both boxes are just as happy holding soil as bearing weight, the same structure doubles as a two-tier garden bed or a stacked bench depending on where it lands in the yard.

The two steps use slightly different lumber on purpose: the bottom step is built from 2x8s while the top step also uses 2x8s, which makes the bottom step 1-1/2" taller than the top — a detail the source is upfront about accepting if the base will be surrounded by grass anyway. If matched step heights matter for your use, swap 2x6s into the bottom step instead, since a plain height mismatch stops being invisible once there's no grass to hide the difference.

Build both boxes with self-tapping deck screws, then set the top step on the bottom and adjust for square before adding the treads and trim. The 45°-mitered 2x4 top trim is the one part that needs real corner-cutting care — everything else is straightforward butt joints in construction lumber. **Use materials genuinely rated for outdoor and ground-contact exposure** — anything less breaks down fast sitting directly on soil or grass.

cutList.note: Swap for 2x6 stock if you want the bottom step to match the top step's height.
Both ends cut at 45°, long point to long point measurement.

steps.title: Build the bottom step
steps.body: Build the bottom step box from the 2x8 long sides and ends, screwing together with self-tapping deck screws. If you need the top and bottom step heights to match exactly, substitute 2x6 stock for the bottom step instead of 2x8.

steps.title: Build the top step
steps.body: Begin the top step construction with the long side, ends, and internal supports as shown, then attach the remaining boards to complete the top step box.

steps.title: Stack and square
steps.body: Place the top step on the bottom step and adjust for square.

steps.title: Attach the trim and treads
steps.body: Attach the tie-in trim and mitered top trim to the steps framing, then attach the treads on top. Finish with materials rated for outdoor and ground-contact use.

images.alt: Two-tier outdoor planter steps built from 2x8 lumber
